nodes.label: AI as learning and study aid
nodes.label_negative: AI as cumbersome overhead for routine or context-heavy tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I will use AI to help when I'm troubleshooting or debugging and my own troubleshooting efforts aren't getting me far

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: Definitely collaboration. I find myself explaining a lot to the AI what I'm trying to achieve

nodes.label: AI integration into workflow
nodes.grounding_span: Where we hope to incorporate AI into our work is with automated tools integrated into our work environment

nodes.label: AI evaluation of domain-specific tasks
nodes.grounding_span: using AI tools is much more effective and helpful when the scope of what the AI tool can handle is more narrow

nodes.label: AI as judgment-free explainer of fundamental concepts
nodes.label_negative: Human expert as source of explanation (with social cost)
nodes.grounding_span: Being able to ask AI stupid, basic questions to really drill in the point helped me understand in a way I'd never have been comfortable to badger a professor

nodes.label: Professional competence
nodes.grounding_span: helped me understand in a way I'd never have been comfortable to badger a professor to understand

nodes.label: Efficiency and speed in work
nodes.grounding_span: it feels impossible to explain all those little peculiarities and feels burdensome

nodes.label: Job security and skill relevance
nodes.grounding_span: It's a scary thought... I imagine AI-Native companies in the industry will change the way employment works

nodes.label: Pragmatic openness to expanding AI use

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool

nodes.label: Worry about AI's negative impact on jobs

nodes.label: Preference for narrow AI over general LLMs
nodes.grounding_span: using AI tools is much more effective and helpful when the scope of what the AI tool can handle is more narrow

nodes.label: Context-dependent tool use — uses AI when alone, colleagues when available
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use AI daily or in a typical work day, but probably once a week or every couple of weeks when I'm working on something particularly challenging or new

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: Definitely collaboration. I find myself explaining a lot to the AI what I'm trying to achieve and what is already there
